programme.title: Sarah Beaney Cup
programme.desc: Live rugby coverage of the Sarah Beaney Cup final as Watsonians take on Hillhead Jordanhill at Murrayfield.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Angus MacMillan talks about old-fashioned fishing methods and shows us how to use a polyboard.

programme.title: Immigration Tracks
programme.desc: This week we experience the European flavour of Quebec City. And Anne follows the St Lawrence River to Montreal.

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Traditional and roots music show presented by Mary Ann Kennedy. Filmed during Celtic Connections 2025, Seirm showcases bespoke recordings and conversations with some of the best artists appearing at the festival. This episode features performances from Loudon Wainwright III, Ímar, Larry Campbell & Teresa Williams, Ablaye Cissoko & Cyrille Brotto and Fell Line.

programme.title: Fàsach na h-Eòrpa
programme.desc: For 9,000 years, the European wild sheep could not be driven from its original territory in Cyprus and Sardinia. However, Poachers almost wiped it out, but the wild ancestor of today's domestic sheep, the mouflon, is a survival artist.

programme.title: Hoolie
programme.desc: Scotland's biggest night of traditional music, Hoolie 2025 celebrates comedy and folk music legend Sir Billy Connolly, featuring a stellar line-up from Scotland's folk and traditional music scene.

programme.desc: Skipinnish le set beothail bho Àrd-Ùrlar Hot House aig Belladrum 2025. Skipinnish with an energetic set from the Hot House Stage at Belladrum.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Meanbh is Beag 'son rudan a cheartachadh an dèidh dhaibh tubaist adbharachadh. Meanbh and Beag cause an accident which involves bubble flowers, the Sky-Moo and the Iron Chicken.

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Tha Linda a' tadhal air Meaban is Moo gus dùbhlan a chur romhpa. An-diugh feumaidh iad rudan a tha dubh a lorg. Linda challenges Meaban and Moo to find black items.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean
programme.desc: Marvin misplaces his favourite rock, and when no substitute will suffice, Oona assists him in retrieving the original from the seagulls.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean
programme.desc: Mama and Papa plan a special family outing for Oona and Baba, but they are still searching for the ideal location.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.desc: Because they are a little short of money, and considering money notes are only little papers with drawings on them, Petit and his friends decide to create their own money and buy anything they want.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach
programme.desc: Brush decides to start a ladies club and invites all the ladies of the forest for brunch. When Fox and Tusk try to join in, dressed as ladies, Brush soon discovers their true identity and kicks them out. Offended, Fox and Tusk decide to start a club.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Arthur Cormack reads `Alfons, chan fhaod thu sin a dhèanamh'.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Stella ag iarraidh a' bhith àbhaisteach ach tha beachd eile aig Màthair na Bànrigh! Stella wants to be like everyone else but the Queen Mother has other ideas. She would like to ensure that Stella isn't normal.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Stella air màirneal fhaighinn an dèidh mi-thuigs' mu thilgeil ball-pàipear. After a misunderstanding over the throwing of a paper ball in assembly, Stella is given a detention for the first time ever.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Kerene loves dancing so much that she is going to start a dance group.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: Sébastien and Belle are inseparable friends. He's a spirited little boy and she's a big white dog who had been abandoned. Exploring the valley and the Alpine peaks where they live, they come to the aid of people in difficulty.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: Bitzer treats the Farmer to a restaurant-style dining experience, offering a delicious pie as the main course. However, when the pie goes missing, Bitzer makes the mistake of accepting Shaun and the flock's offer of help in the kitchen.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Scottish Women's Premier League
programme.desc: Ball-coise beò bhon SWPL le Partick Thistle a' cur fàilte air Glasgow City. An geama slàn còmhla ri Micheal Steele agus Ailig O'Henley. Live football action from the SWPL as Partick Thistle host Glasgow City. Full match coverage in the company of presenter Micheal Steele and commentator Alex O'Henley.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Tha Abigail a' sealltainn dhuinn mar a bhios i a' dèanamh waffles, biadh blasta airson brunch. Abigail takes us through her process for making waffles, a tasty brunch experience.

programme.title: Steelmen
programme.desc: In 1991, as the town of Motherwell faced the crippling social, economic and cultural impact of the closure of the Ravenscraig steelworks, its football club, locally known as the Steelmen, lifted the prestigious Scottish Cup for the first time in 39 years. The victory was a glimmer of light in the ensuing darkness.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Katie MacRishnidh fishes with Angus and talks about the role fishing plays in the life of the island community.

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A deep-dive into the BBC's archives to celebrate the great Roy Orbison, full-time rock `n' roll legend, part-time Traveling Wilbury and the man Elvis famously described as `the best singer in the world'.

programme.title: Roy Orbison and Friends: A Black and White Night
programme.desc: The iconic musician Roy Orbison performs a set of his greatest hits, such as `Pretty Woman' and `Crying', backed by an eclectic ensemble of stars including Tom Waits and Bruce Springsteen.

programme.title: Roy Sings Orbison
programme.desc: Roy Orbison sings some of his greatest hits, including `Only the Lonely', `Crying', `Penny Arcade', `Blue Bayou', `Running Scared', `Candy Man', `In Dreams', `Mean Woman Blues', `It's Over' and `Oh, Pretty Woman'.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: One man transfixed television viewers during snooker's golden age - his name was Alex `Hurricane' Higgins. This poignant documentary charts the life of snooker's first genius, from his early days growing up in Belfast to his climb to the top of the sport as two-time world champion. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Gold Run
programme.desc: In 1940, the Germans invaded Norway with clear goals in mind: the king, the government, to control the coast, the iron ore and the Norwegian gold reserves. At the same time, a group of resistance fighters was given a most important and secret mission: save Norway's gold.

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Escapologist Harry Houdini was one of the icons of the early 20th century. Restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists recall his extraordinary impact on audiences and reveal the secrets behind some of his most amazing escapes.

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Professor Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary and revolutionary periods in British history: the Industrial Revolution. He explains the unique economic, social and political conditions that by the 19th century, led to Britain becoming the richest, most powerful nation on the planet. It was a time that transformed the way people think, work and play forever.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: One man transfixed television viewers during snooker's golden age - his name was Alex `Hurricane' Higgins. This poignant documentary charts the life of snooker's first genius, from his early days growing up in Belfast to his climb to the top of the sport as two-time world champion. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In the Gomantong cave system, Steve Backshall encounters thousands of cockroaches and one of the scariest creepy crawlies to go on the `Deadly 60' list, the scutigera centipede. Thousands of wrinkle-lipped bats call this cave their home. It is a truly awesome sight as in their thousands, they leave the cave to hunt for bugs in the night sky.

programme.title: Despicable Me
programme.desc: When his wickedness is challenged, super-villain Gru hatches a plan to steal the Moon. Enlisting three young orphans to help out, his dastardly plot is derailed when he begins to care for them.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: After a blackout causes havoc, the team trace the source to a substation and discover an electrocuted body. On first appearances, it seems like an accident, but further investigation reveals a plot involving cryptocurrency and stolen electricity.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: DS Janie Mallowan teams up with eccentric national treasure and beloved TV detective John Chapel, to solve a murder.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: After a mysterious walker is found dead on a Welsh mountain trail, Janie ropes John into joining her mam Yvonne's walking group to investigate the unidentified woman.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's friends and family gather round when his boyfriend dumps him on the doorstep! Can he fight for his love and win back his man?

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Sick of dating creeps, Lucinda is given the royal princess treatment courtesy of her fairy godmother. Sal auditions for a talent show, and Dickie offers sage advice in the work place.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast from Northern Irish creator rarewitch. The cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe. Surreal, chaotic, and hilariously absurd - welcome to the madcap world of Glue.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The documentarian and journalist exposes issues affecting young people around the world. From Bulgaria, to across Europe in the Roma Gypsy community.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The documentarian and journalist exposes issues affecting young people around the world. From Bulgaria, to across Europe in the Roma Gypsy community.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey heads to Bedfordshire to the private site where 23-year-old Chantelle lives with her husband, Swayley. Chantelle prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her granny Rita taught her and sharing them on social media.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain the shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand what's causing this high-street epidemic, Stacey is meeting the people behind the crime and spending time on the shop floor with the staff who deal with shoplifting every day. In Manchester city centre, beauty and cosmetics chain Bodycare has one of its biggest and most shoplifted stores.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Orbiting a quiet backwater planet, the massed forces of the universe's deadliest species gather, drawn to a mysterious message that echoes out to the stars. The Doctor must learn what this signal means for his own fate and that of the universe.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's Christmas at Button House and Mike and Alison are heading to Mike's parents to celebrate with family. As Mike piles up the Tupperware with what he insists are only side dishes, despite one clearly being an entire turkey, Kitty expresses her disappointment at not spending Christmas day with Alison. Alison promises that they'll be back by the evening and what's more, reveals to the ghosts that there is a Christmas surprise for each of them in the library.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's coming up to Christmas, and Mike's mum is staying. Betty quickly overstays her welcome, but Alison and Mike don't quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isn't your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Miranda's joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides it's time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so he'll see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than he'd originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Ken's house of all his junk or as Ken calls it, his memories. As Ken's suspicions over Richard's true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob and Richard's former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard offers to take Ken to the local golf club, unaware that he's about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than he'd originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists in order to secure Ken a doctor's appointment and is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and endeavours to track down Ken's oldest friend, who Ken lost touch with years ago, in order to invite him to Ken's surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jean's ashes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny is reeling after being chatted up by Louise's Dad. He decides to become more macho, but his plan backfires with shocking consequences.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is reeling that Gaz tried to take Corinthian and decides to increase security in the home, while Jonny gets a job as a farmer. Elsewhere, Donna persuades Gaz to try tantric sex, but he gets hooked on it - much to her frustration, and Louise wants a new family and she knows exactly where to find one.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Orbiting a quiet backwater planet, the massed forces of the universe's deadliest species gather, drawn to a mysterious message that echoes out to the stars. The Doctor must learn what this signal means for his own fate and that of the universe.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs to be done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the day's game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tries the deadly delicacy of puffer fish in the harbour town of Shimonoseki and visits Hitachi's Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There's a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will literally bear fruit later in the year.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Sue Kent is busy in her Swansea garden, and Jamie Butterworth learns why the euphorbia is a bright star of the border.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George's Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman introduces the energetic Suzi Ruffell and South African comedian Loyiso Gola.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Don't Look Now
programme.desc: A woman has recurring visions of her dead daughter while travelling to Venice with her husband. They meet two elderly sisters, one of whom informs the mother that she can contact her dead daughter.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Highlights from the seventh day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Current affairs programme, featuring interviews and investigative reports on all aspects of life in Britain.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George's Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Millie hosts a series of tough penguin games, Pancake and Nugget must stick together.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard race against time to retrieve sun googles so they can watch a cosmic triple eclipse.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are made to clean the Cave of Mirrors as punishment for frolicking before a quest is complete, and they accidentally release the Party Harpy.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings use cans and tins to build a beach slide, which annoys Grizzy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse's light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Life is back to normal for Roy and friends, until wild Pokémon begin to act strangely...

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe become pop stars but have to do as the other says, then become their dogs!

programme.desc: Will the horsing around, a piano break, a worm or a complimentary banana prove too much?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of the king of snakes and the endangered loris.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Horticulturists offer various techniques to elevate the home gardening experience, from repositioning roses in rustic layouts to planning vibrant spring displays.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage of day 8 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: How does a chimpanzee see the world? A research project at Edinburgh Zoo is designed to answer just that question in an innovative new way - by training chimps to use video touch screens and giving them a special chimp-proof camera. How will they react to tools which in evolutionary terms are a few million years ahead of them?

programme.title: Women's Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage as Wales take on England in the Women's Six Nations.

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest news, analysis, reaction and interviews from the afternoon's biggest football matches.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by experts Michael Baggott and Claire Rawle at Longleat in Wiltshire. Claire makes a momentous discovery of Tibetan artefacts belonging to a collection of controversial historical significance, which all end up under the hammer.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: A financier travelling from Istanbul to Paris is found dead, stabbed a dozen times, and it is up to celebrated Belgian detective Hercule Poirot to solve the crime. He painstakingly interviews those in the same railway car and through an elaborate re-creation aims to find out just who carried out the deed.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys crisscrossed by clear rivers. Its western border adjoins Kielder Forest, one of the largest man-made forests in Europe.

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: DJ Spoony selects a playlist of 40 great tracks that bring together some of the most inspiring names from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage.

programme.title: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: To be advised.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Highlights from day 8 of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon finally meets a boy she likes. But dating is always tough when you're the only daughter of a crime family. When her parents realise who Arran is, will she fight for him?

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of McCartney's original Höfner bass over 50 years ago is one of rock 'n' roll's great mysteries. This is the story of the iconic instrument and the quest to find it.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith are in the Peak District National Park, famed for its breathtaking landscapes and wildlife.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs to be done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the day's game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tries the deadly delicacy of puffer fish in the harbour town of Shimonoseki and visits Hitachi's Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There's a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will literally bear fruit later in the year.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Sue Kent is busy in her Swansea garden, and Jamie Butterworth learns why the euphorbia is a bright star of the border.

programme.desc: It's the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George's Hall, Liverpool. programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are made to clean the Cave of Mirrors as punishment for frolicking before a quest is complete, and they accidentally release the Party Harpy.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings use cans and tins to build a beach slide, which annoys Grizzy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse's light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Life is back to normal for Roy and friends, until wild Pokémon begin to act strangely...

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe become pop stars but have to do as the other says, then become their dogs!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Will the horsing around, a piano break, a worm or a complimentary banana prove too much?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of the king of snakes and the endangered loris.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Sue Kent is busy in her Swansea garden, and Jamie Butterworth learns why the euphorbia is a bright star of the border.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage of day 8 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: How does a chimpanzee see the world? A research project at Edinburgh Zoo is designed to answer just that question in an innovative new way - by training chimps to use video touch screens and giving them a special chimp-proof camera. How will they react to tools which in evolutionary terms are a few million years ahead of them?

programme.title: Women's Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage as Wales take on England in the Women's Six Nations.

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest news, analysis, reaction and interviews from the afternoon's biggest football matches.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by experts Michael Baggott and Claire Rawle at Longleat in Wiltshire. Claire makes a momentous discovery of Tibetan artefacts belonging to a collection of controversial historical significance, which all end up under the hammer.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: A financier travelling from Istanbul to Paris is found dead, stabbed a dozen times, and it is up to celebrated Belgian detective Hercule Poirot to solve the crime. He painstakingly interviews those in the same railway car and through an elaborate re-creation aims to find out just who carried out the deed.

programme.title: Women's Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Full match coverage as Ireland face France in the Women's Six Nations. Join BBC Sport NI for comprehensive expert commentary, in-depth analysis and all the reaction before and after the final whistle.

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: DJ Spoony selects a playlist of 40 great tracks that bring together some of the most inspiring names from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Highlights from day 8 of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon finally meets a boy she likes. But dating is always tough when you're the only daughter of a crime family. When her parents realise who Arran is, will she fight for him?

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of McCartney's original Höfner bass over 50 years ago is one of rock 'n' roll's great mysteries. This is the story of the iconic instrument and the quest to find it.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith are in the Peak District National Park, famed for its breathtaking landscapes and wildlife.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs to be done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the day's game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: World Championship Snooker
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tries the deadly delicacy of puffer fish in the harbour town of Shimonoseki and visits Hitachi's Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There's a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will literally bear fruit later in the year.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Sue Kent is busy in her Swansea garden, and Jamie Butterworth learns why the euphorbia is a bright star of the border.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George's Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman introduces the energetic Suzi Ruffell and South African comedian Loyiso Gola.

programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Don't Look Now
programme.desc: A woman has recurring visions of her dead daughter while travelling to Venice with her husband. They meet two elderly sisters, one of whom informs the mother that she can contact her dead daughter.

programme.title: Insect Worlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the connections and relationship that we have with insects and other arthropods. In Kenya huge armies of driver ants give houses a five-star clean up and in China we discover how silkworm caterpillars have shaped our culture and distribution. While locusts devastate crops in Africa, bees and beetles across the world provide a key link in our food chains.

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan is at the Running Show to look ahead to the 46th London Marathon, which promises to set a world record for the biggest ever staged.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs to be done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: One man transfixed television viewers during snooker's golden age - his name was Alex `Hurricane' Higgins. This poignant documentary charts the life of snooker's first genius, from his early days growing up in Belfast to his climb to the top of the sport as two-time world champion. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Gold Run
programme.desc: In 1940, the Germans invaded Norway with clear goals in mind: the king, the government, to control the coast, the iron ore and the Norwegian gold reserves. At the same time, a group of resistance fighters was given a most important and secret mission: save Norway's gold.

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Escapologist Harry Houdini was one of the icons of the early 20th century. Restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists recall his extraordinary impact on audiences and reveal the secrets behind some of his most amazing escapes.

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Professor Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary and revolutionary periods in British history: the Industrial Revolution. He explains the unique economic, social and political conditions that by the 19th century, led to Britain becoming the richest, most powerful nation on the planet. It was a time that transformed the way people think, work and play forever.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion

Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.desc: In the Gomantong cave system, Steve Backshall encounters thousands of cockroaches and one of the scariest creepy crawlies to go on the `Deadly 60' list, the scutigera centipede. programme.title: Despicable Me

programme.title: Smoggie Queens

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain the shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand what's causing this high-street epidemic, Stacey is meeting the people behind the crime and spending time on the shop floor with the staff who deal with shoplifting every day. In Manchester city centre, beauty and cosmetics chain Bodycare has one of its biggest and most shoplifted stores.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's Christmas at Button House and Mike and Alison are heading to Mike's parents to celebrate with family. As Mike piles up the Tupperware with what he insists are only side dishes, despite one clearly being an entire turkey, Kitty expresses her disappointment at not spending Christmas day with Alison. Alison promises that they'll be back by the evening and what's more, reveals to the ghosts that there is a Christmas surprise for each of them in the library.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's coming up to Christmas, and Mike's mum is staying. Betty quickly overstays her welcome, but Alison and Mike don't quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isn't your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Miranda's joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides it's time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so he'll see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than he'd originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Ken's house of all his junk or as Ken calls it, his memories. As Ken's suspicions over Richard's true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob and Richard's former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard offers to take Ken to the local golf club, unaware that he's about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than he'd originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child

programme.title: Only Child

programme.title: Only Child

If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: Tagessegen
programme.desc: Pfarrer Heinz Förg spendet täglich den Segen und liest dazu eine passende Bibelstelle.

programme.title: Bibel TV das Gespräch
programme.desc: Tilmann Geske war Übersetzer in der Osttürkei und arbeitete an einer türkischen Studienbibel. Am 18. April 2007 wurde er von fünf jungen Männern ermordet. Seine Frau Susanne Geske hat seine Geschichte niedergeschrieben.

programme.title: IDEA TV
programme.desc: In diesem Nachrichtenformat wird täglich über Neuigkeiten aus der christlichen Welt berichtet.

programme.title: Die Faszination der Ölmalerei
programme.desc: Gary zeigt, wie man mit Öl Landschaften, Blumen und Tiere auf die Leinwand zaubern kann. Er kann in 30 Minuten ein Gemälde entstehen lassen und führt den Zuschauer dabei Schritt für Schritt durch den Prozess bis zum fertigen Bild.

programme.title: Bibel TV Sing Mit!
programme.desc: Choräle, Kirchen- und Glaubenslieder moderner Interpreten werden vorgestellt und laden ein zum Mitsingen. Die Liedtexte sind eingeblendet, sodass das Mitsingen leicht fällt. Es soll Spaß machen und mit Freude erfüllen.

programme.title: Ein Wunder für jeden Tag
programme.desc: In dieser Sendung wird eine tägliche Kurzandacht mit Déborah Rosenkranz präsentiert.

programme.title: Erlebt TV
programme.desc: Zu einer Ehe gehört es, gemeinsam Krisen und Nöte zu durchstehen. Drei junge Paare erzählen, wie sie dies in ihrer Ehe schon sehr früh erlebten.

programme.title: Alpha & Omega
programme.desc: Gezeigt wird Kirchenmagazin der Diözese Rottenburg-Stuttgart, der Erzdiözese Freiburg und der Landeskirche in Württemberg. Die Sendung behandelt in lockerer Gesprächsrunde und mit Filmbeiträgen aktuelle Themen aus Kirche und Gesellschaft.

programme.title: Drive Thru History - Unterwegs ..
programme.desc: In der Zeit von Jesu Geburt war die politische Stimmung aufgeheizt. Die Juden lebten in römischer Unterdrückung und erwarteten ihren Messias. Sie träumten von einem mächtigen König, der sie befreien sollte. Aber wie würde man ihn erkennen?

programme.title: Kirche neu erleben - mit Tobias Teichen
programme.desc: Tobias geht grundlegenden Fragen nach und versucht anhand der Bibel den richtigen Weg zu finden.

programme.title: So lebt sich's gut
programme.desc: Von manchen wie Unkraut verkannt und abgelehnt, für andere wie eine Blume gewollt: Die Bibel sei wie Löwenzahn - sie ist nicht aufzuhalten und bricht sogar durch Asphaltschichten durch - sagt Doris Schulte in ihrer Andacht.

programme.title: Geschichten von Gottes Wirken - Meilensteine im Dienst für verfolgte Christen
programme.desc: Der Leiter von "Open Doors", Markus Rode, erzählt von seinem Berufsweg, der ihn aus einer weltlichen Karriere in eine göttliche Berufung führte. Warum er in eine Identitätskrise kam und wie er Gottes Stimme hörte, berichtet er in diesem Interview.

programme.title: Fenster zum Sonntag
programme.desc: Ein dumpfes Grollen in den Bergen. Ein Mensch verschwindet spurlos. Rätselhaft endet ein Leben. Staatsanwältin Gabriela Bürgi sucht zwischen Akten, abgelegenen Pfaden und den Spuren menschlicher Schicksale nach Antworten - und nach Gerechtigkeit.

programme.title: Bibel TV DIE SENDUNG
programme.desc: Bibel TV Geschäftsführer Matthias Brender informiert über Aktuelles aus der Redaktion.

programme.title: Lebensweisen
programme.desc: Karnik Gregorian und Tamara Häußler-Eisenmann begeben sich vielerorts auf die Suche nach den kleinen, spannenden Geschichten, die das Leben schreibt - sei es auf dem Fußballfeld, einem Bio-Bauernhof oder einem Mutter-Kind-Haus.

programme.title: Erlebt TV
programme.desc: Die Sendung geht den Fragen auf den Grund, ob es Gott gibt und ob man Gott erfahren kann. Erzählt werden spannende, berührende und unglaubliche Geschichten von Menschen, deren Leben durch eine Begegnung mit Gott auf den Kopf gestellt wurde.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer: Das Leben genießen
programme.desc: Die amerikanische Bibellehrerin Joyce Meyer nimmt kein Blatt vor den Mund. Humorvoll und direkt spricht sie die Herausforderungen an, die Menschen heutzutage im Alltag begegnen. Sie bietet praktische Lebenshilfe, die auf der Bibel basiert.

programme.title: Frei sein mit Wilkin van de Kamp
domradio.de - Christliche Nachrichten
programme.desc: Diese Sendung präsentiert christliche Nachrichten und Aktuelles aus der Kirche und aller Welt.

programme.title: Avonlea - Das Mädchen aus der Stadt
programme.desc: Der Geschäftsmann und Sara Stanleys Vater Blair hat aus finanziellen Gründen Schwierigkeiten, sich weiter um seiner Tochter zu kümmern. Daher schickt er die zehnjährige Sara zu der Familie seiner verstorbenen Ehefrau in der Stadt von Avonlea.

programme.title: Ein Hauch von Himmel
programme.desc: Sandy ist Radiomoderatorin und tut alles für die Quote. Ihr besonderes Markenzeichen sind Beleidigungen, Erniedrigungen und freche Sprüche. Nicht selten nutzt sie auch das Leben ihrer Tochter als Material in ihrer Sendung.

programme.title: Bibel TV Emmaus
programme.desc: "Emmaus" ist die Andacht zum Tag. Sie möchte Inspiration geben und den Glauben stärken, wie in der Geschichte von den beiden Jüngern, die auf dem Weg nach Emmaus waren und auf ihrer Reise die Größe Jesu erlebten und tief ergriffen waren.

programme.title: IDEA TV
programme.desc: In diesem Nachrichtenformat wird täglich über Neuigkeiten aus der christlichen Welt berichtet.

programme.title: Don Matteo
programme.desc: Don Matteo ist ein Priester, der mit der Polizei zusammenarbeitet und gemeinsam versuchen, sie Verbrechen zu lösen. Mit seinem Wissen über die verschiedenen Wesen der Menschen ist er eine ziemlich große Hilfe für die Polizei.

programme.title: Lebenswege
programme.desc: Der Moderator interviewt Menschen aus unterschiedlichsten Bereichen in der Schweiz.

programme.title: GRANDIOS
programme.desc: Verschiedene Interviews mit Menschen des christlichen Magazins "Grandios" werden gezeigt.

programme.title: Weltbeweger
programme.desc: Auf Gran Canaria im GoGlobal Jüngerschaftszentrum Cambio durchlaufen junge Menschen aus Spanien und Deutschland gemeinsam eine persönliche Wachstumszeit. Mitarbeitende und Teilnehmende erzählen in der Sendung von ihren Erlebnissen.

programme.title: Kirche neu erleben - mit Tobias Teichen
programme.desc: Tobias geht grundlegenden Fragen nach und versucht anhand der Bibel den richtigen Weg zu finden.

programme.desc: In diesem Nachrichtenformat wird täglich über Neuigkeiten aus der christlichen Welt berichtet.

programme.title: Superfromm
programme.desc: Thomas Meyerhöfer bespricht mit seinen Gästen, dass ein Leben mit Jesus nicht immer einfach ist.

programme.title: Bibel TV DIE SENDUNG
programme.desc: Bibel TV Geschäftsführer Matthias Brender informiert über Aktuelles aus der Redaktion.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer: Das Leben genießen
programme.desc: Die amerikanische Bibellehrerin Joyce Meyer nimmt kein Blatt vor den Mund. Humorvoll und direkt spricht sie die Herausforderungen an, die Menschen heutzutage im Alltag begegnen. Sie bietet praktische Lebenshilfe, die auf der Bibel basiert.

programme.title: Die Bibel - Esther
programme.desc: Xerxes, der mächtige König von Persien, verstößt seine Gattin Waschti, weil sie sich nicht seinem Willen beugen will. Auf der Suche nach einer neuen Ehefrau trifft er die bezaubernde Esther. Ohne von ihrer jüdischen Herkunft zu wissen, heiratet er die charmante Schönheit. Doch Esthers Tage sind gezählt. Haman, der erste Minister des Reichs, plant per Gesetz die Ermordung der im persischen Königreich lebenden Juden.

programme.title: Bibel TV Emmaus
programme.desc: Paulus wird öfter als Vorzeigechrist gesehen. Christen soll man ansehen, dass sie Jesus nachfolgen. Doch was heißt das, ein authentisches Leben in Christus zu führen? Mark-Torsten Wardein vom Missionswerk Neues Leben antwortet in 14 Punkten.

programme.title: In Touch mit Dr. Charles Stanley

programme.desc: Daniel Müller beleuchtet sieben wichtige Aspekte der Heilung und wie man sie empfangen kann.

programme.desc: Es werden Konzerte bekannter christlicher Musiker präsentiert. Auch Newcomer sind dabei. Eine bunte Auswahl von christlicher Musik wird vorgestellt und begeistert die Zuschauer.

programme.title: Home Church Message
programme.desc: Die Vision der "Home Church" ist, dass die Menschen Gottes Träume für ihr Leben entdecken. Das wird auch in ihren Gottesdiensten deutlich. Das Pastoren-Team arbeitet mit Leidenschaft daran, diese Vision für eine Kirche Realität werden zu lassen.

programme.title: Ein Wunder für jeden Tag
programme.desc: In dieser Sendung wird eine tägliche Kurzandacht mit Déborah Rosenkranz präsentiert.

programme.title: Hour of Power

programme.title: HOPE & LIFE Coffee Talk
programme.desc: Konrad Blaser erzählt, wie Gott in der Hope & Life Church wirkt und seine Mitmenschen bewegt.

programme.title: Weitersagen. Beten. Spenden
programme.desc: Petra Blümel, die als Christin in der DDR gelebt hat, ist schon lange ein Fan von Bibel TV.

programme.title: Frei sein mit Wilkin van de Kamp
Ein Hauch von Himmel
programme.desc: Thomas, der Sohn von Monica Kate Prescott, kehrt zu ihr zurück. Er hat jetzt sogar einen eigenen Sohn. Ihr blindes Vertrauen in Thomas bringt Kate dazu, ihm eine Menge Geld zu leihen, das er angeblich in einen neuen Job investieren will.

programme.title: Ein Hauch von Himmel
programme.desc: Als in der Stadt Aynesville ein Mann aufgrund seiner Hautfarbe ermordet wird, verliert Tess die Hoffnung in die Menschheit. Monica meldet den Tod der Polizei, doch die beiden Officer werden vom Stadtrat daran gehindert, in dem Mordfall zu ermitteln.

programme.title: Don Matteo
programme.desc: Don Matteo ist ein Priester, der mit der Polizei zusammenarbeitet und gemeinsam versuchen, sie Verbrechen zu lösen. Mit seinem Wissen über die verschiedenen Wesen der Menschen ist er eine ziemlich große Hilfe für die Polizei.

programme.title: Superfromm
programme.desc: Thomas Meyerhöfer bespricht mit seinen Gästen, dass ein Leben mit Jesus nicht immer einfach ist.

programme.title: Fokus Jerusalem
programme.desc: Israel, das Land der Bibel, ist auch ein Land der Konflikte. Ob zwischen Palästinensern und Israeliten oder Christen, Juden und Muslimen, es kommt immer wieder zu Auseinandersetzungen. Informiert wird mit aktuellen Beiträgen und Kommentaren.

programme.title: ERF: Mensch, Gott!

programme.title: Wesley und die Gebete
programme.desc: Ein Junge betet und setzt alles daran, dass Gott seiner schwerkranken Schwester hilft.

programme.title: Luis Palau - Die jungen Jahre des weltbekannten Predigers
Fußball mit Vision
programme.desc: Die Profi-Fußballspielerin Giovanna Hoffmann und die Bundesligaprofis Felix Uduokhai sowie Maxence Lacroix berichten in der Sendung, wie sie persönlich zum Glauben gefunden haben und durch Gott zu einem neuen Menschen wurden.

programme.title: Ein Engel auf Erden
programme.desc: Jonathan und Mark arbeiten als Aushilfslehrer an einer Highschool. Jonathan unterrichtet Geschichte und Mark trainiert eine Mädchenvolleyballmannschaft. Der Abschlussball steht bevor und die Engel helfen den gestressten Schülern.

programme.title: Ein Engel auf Erden
programme.desc: Gail hat ein Buch über ihre Mutter Phoebe Hall, eine berühmte Hollywoodschauspielerin, geschrieben, in dem sie diese als Rabenmutter bloßstellt. Mark und Jonathan erhalten die Aufgabe, Mutter und Tochter wieder zusammenzubringen.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer: Das Leben genießen
programme.desc: Die amerikanische Bibellehrerin Joyce Meyer nimmt kein Blatt vor den Mund. Humorvoll und direkt spricht sie die Herausforderungen an, die Menschen heutzutage im Alltag begegnen. Sie bietet praktische Lebenshilfe, die auf der Bibel basiert.

programme.title: Das Matthäus-Evangelium
programme.desc: Johannes der Täufer lässt aus dem Gefängnis fragen, ob Jesus der verheißene Messias ist. Jesus spricht daraufhin über die Bedeutung Johannes' des Täufers. Er beklagt, dass viele Menschen trotz der Wunder, die er getan hat, nicht an ihn glauben.

programme.desc: In der Talkshow wird mit drei Gästen über diverse christliche und alltägliche Themen diskutiert.

programme.desc: "Emmaus" ist die Andacht zum Tag. Sie möchte Inspiration geben und den Glauben stärken, wie in der Geschichte von den beiden Jüngern, die auf dem Weg nach Emmaus waren und auf ihrer Reise die Größe Jesu erlebten und tief ergriffen waren.

programme.title: Die Bibel - Esther
programme.desc: Xerxes, der mächtige König von Persien, verstößt seine Gattin Waschti, weil sie sich nicht seinem Willen beugen will. Auf der Suche nach einer neuen Ehefrau trifft er die bezaubernde Esther. Ohne von ihrer jüdischen Herkunft zu wissen, heiratet er die charmante Schönheit. Doch Esthers Tage sind gezählt. Haman, der erste Minister des Reichs, plant per Gesetz die Ermordung der im persischen Königreich lebenden Juden.
